_comment: Binds the committed confidential SP1 guest ELFs to the verifying keys they must be deployed with. Two ELFs from one `cargo prove build`: elf/cxfer-guest (the settle guest → program_vkey, immutable PROGRAM_VKEY) and elf/reflection-prover (the Bitcoin-state relay prover → bitcoin_relay_vkey, immutable BITCOIN_RELAY_VKEY). verify-vkey-pin.sh asserts both sha256s on every PR. A rebuild that changes either ELF MUST regenerate its vkey + update this file IN THE SAME COMMIT, then redeploy ConfidentialPool with the new immutable vkey — otherwise every settle (resp. every attestBitcoinStateProven) reverts. Each vkey is the key a REAL Groth16 proof of THAT ELF verifies against on-chain (test/ConfidentialSwapProofReal.t.sol, test/ConfidentialReflectionProofReal.t.sol). The two share cxfer-core, so a cxfer-core change rebuilds BOTH — committing the ELFs is what keeps the prover box running the pinned bytes rather than a drifting rebuild.

_staged_reprove_2026_06_17._note: STAGED, NOT PROMOTED (uncommitted, for review): the canonical vkeys for the launch re-prove, produced via `cargo prove build --docker` (sp1 toolchain 150e629, image v6.2.2). The AUTHORITATIVE fields above + the deployed Sepolia contract still carry the FROZEN current vkeys (program 0x00d5b572 / reflection 0x007a9fee) — DO NOT promote standalone: rotating without a coordinated redeploy breaks the live reflection (verifies 0x007a9fee), the *ProofReal fixtures (proofs vs the old vkeys), and the readiness-gate layer-9 allowlist. The launch commit (PLAN-unified-twochain-rollout.md) promotes these into program_vkey/bitcoin_relay_vkey + reflection_elf_sha256/elf_sha256 + *_bytes, swaps elf/cxfer-guest + elf/reflection-prover for the docker builds at contracts/sp1/confidential/target/elf-compilation/docker/riscv64im-succinct-zkvm-elf/release/{confidential-pool-prover,reflection-prover}, updates verify-vkey-pin.sh FROZEN_* + the readiness-gate allowlist, regenerates BOTH *ProofReal fixtures (box GPU), sets DeployConfidentialPool DEFAULT_VKEY, and redeploys — one commit, with the PV / lock-set / refund-gate change.
_staged_reprove_2026_06_17.source: option-a lp/cBTC on-chain opening blindings + Mode-B consumed_count digest term + the staged settle adaptor-swap op-set (OP_ADAPTOR_LOCK/CLAIM/REFUND)
_staged_reprove_2026_06_17.reproducibility: reflection vkey CONFIRMED reproducible: the local `cargo prove build --docker` ELF is byte-identical (sha d5fd5013) to the vast-box native-Linux `cargo prove build`; the canonical build env is LINUX (a macOS-host non-docker build diverged to 0x000a0599).
_staged_reprove_2026_06_17._caveat_finding1_anchor_2026_06_17: DO NOT promote staged_bitcoin_relay_vkey as-is: it predates the eth-reflection cross-cycle anchor. The reflection guest now binds the eth proof's committed priorDigest into the resume-digest chain (reflect.rs `expected_prior`), with cxfer-core ScanReflection.eth_refl_digest folded into digest() and the eth-reflection guest committing prior/new digest words; this also moved the reflection genesis digest to eab17bcb92a60d3504973e52dad54aaafe331d87999f94304fac7c29cf126388 (contract REFLECTION_GENESIS_DIGEST + JS SCAN_GENESIS now match it). The staged `source` lists the consumed_count digest term but NOT this set-content anchor, so the reflection ELF MUST be rebuilt from current source before promotion — expect a bitcoin_relay_vkey != 0x00d06eda and the genesis digest above. The staged settle vkey 0x007e4c09 carries the committed cross-lane reads + adaptor ops; confirm its build snapshot covers them when promoting.

_vkey_authority: program_vkey + bitcoin_relay_vkey are the AUTHORITATIVE deploy vkeys for the committed ELFs; guest_state below is descriptive prose only (it has lagged the fields before). The vkey<->ELF binding is enforced mechanically at prove time by the EXPECT_VKEY / ELF_VKEY_PIN guard in exec-prove.rs (program_vkey) and exec-reflect-prove.rs (bitcoin_relay_vkey), and at deploy time by DeployConfidentialPool's require(vkey == .program_vkey). After any ELF rebuild, derive both vkeys on the prover host and reconcile every field here in the same commit.
_pending_reprove: FINALIZED 2026-06-14 — MAINNET RE-PROVE STAGED (the live Sepolia deploy vkeys below stay frozen + valid). reflect.rs SOURCE is now ahead of the committed reflection ELF (sha a25f9003, vkey 0x005e6adc, both UNCHANGED + still valid on Sepolia): it gained the TAC burn-deposit dispatch (verify_provenance over a pre-anchor provenance chain) PLUS the mintable cmint-deposit dispatch (valid_leaves = C_0 + issuer-authorized cmints via verify_cmint_authorized; the issuer message is bound byte-for-byte to the live dapp computeMintMsg so a mint the dapp accepts is bridgeable; native-exec-validated on a locally-built ELF: a cmint-rooted note BURN FOLDED, the fixed-supply baseline folds, a tampered issuer sig folds nothing), compile-verified via cargo prove build, which rotates BITCOIN_RELAY_VKEY at the next re-prove. The mainnet re-prove (ops/CHECKLIST-mainnet-reprove.md + ops/DESIGN-trustless-asset-onboarding.md) bundles these dispatches + the mainnet config (ETH_GENESIS_SYNC_COMMITTEE / ETH_REFLECTION_VKEY / Bitcoin genesis anchor); settle 0x00d5b572 is mainnet-config-independent, unchanged unless settle changes. The committed reflection ELF below contains (Sepolia finalization): asset-preservation + per-output vout-derivation + the AMM constant-product consolidation (createPair/isqrt first-mint/min-share LP/OP_SWAP_ROUTE/per-op deadline) + Mode B reverse-bridge recursion (reflection verify_sp1_proof against the eth-reflection guest) + cBTC.zk (fold_cbtc_lock self-custody + the digest-bound cbtcBackingSats 10th PV field). The committed reflection ELF (sha a25f9003) was built deterministically (cargo-prove cfb5544) from the source AS OF the cBTC re-prove; the reflection source has SINCE advanced (the burn-deposit + cmint-deposit onboarding dispatch above), so committed reflect.rs/burn_deposit.rs/bitcoin.rs are now AHEAD of the committed reflection ELF, pending the staged re-prove. The committed SETTLE source is now AHEAD of the committed settle ELF: it gained the adaptor-swap op-set (OP_ADAPTOR_LOCK/CLAIM/REFUND, ops 12-14; ops/DESIGN-adaptor-swap-guest.md) — the cBTC<->BTC atomic bridge + cross-chain orderbook leg — which rotates PROGRAM_VKEY at the launch re-prove. The launch bundle re-proves BOTH ELFs together (the consolidated A0 in PLAN-unified-twochain-rollout.md: reflection gets multiasset Track B/C provenance, settle gets the adaptor ops); the matching ConfidentialPool PublicValues + lock-set accumulator + refund-gate change lands in that SAME commit (adding PV fields earlier would break the committed *ProofReal abi.decode). PROGRAM_VKEY 0x00d5b572 and BITCOIN_RELAY_VKEY 0x005e6adc are FROZEN: the reflection genesis sync-committee anchor is 0x8a83300119ac1e64a2318d3db330ed496c51276c636a93633b2d5cfd283c2d44 (Sepolia finalizedSlot 10462624), frozen for this deploy — re-anchoring is a future weak-subjectivity op that would rotate the vkey. The freeze is mechanically enforced: verify-vkey-pin.sh asserts the reflection leg == FROZEN_REFLECTION_VKEY/SHA + the reflection_groth16 fixture vkey, and readiness-gate layer 9 allowlists 0x005e6adc only with its both-sided REFLECT-1 confirmation. Neither vkey can rotate except by a deliberate coordinated re-prove (rebuild BOTH ELFs -> new vkeys -> regenerate all *ProofReal + this pin + the drift guard FROZEN_* + the allowlist + the DeployConfidentialPool DEFAULT_VKEY, in one commit). Deploy locks PROGRAM_VKEY + BITCOIN_RELAY_VKEY immutable.
guest_state: Settle guest (cxfer-guest, 0x00d5b572, sha c49ba0d3): gen-1 op set + OP_SWAP (fee-enforced clearing) + OP_LP_ADD (isqrt geometric-mean first-mint + V2 min-share) / OP_LP_REMOVE (in-guest MINIMUM_LIQUIDITY floor) + OP_SWAP_ROUTE (<=4-hop constant-product multihop) + OP_OTC + OP_BID; per-op DEADLINE (Expired); createPair lazy pools; pool_id binds the canonical pair + feeBps; amounts bound by opening sigmas. Reflection prover (reflection-prover, 0x007a9fee, sha 1173ede8; FORWARD RE-PROVE 2026-06-15 rotated it off 0x005e6adc with the mode_b verify_sp1_proof gate): full-scan witnessed Bitcoin-state relay + REFLECT-1 value-conservation + CXFER asset-preservation + per-output vout-derivation + Mode B reverse-bridge recursion (verify_sp1_proof against the eth-reflection guest hash_u32 vkey, genesis anchor 0x8a83…) + cBTC.zk fold_cbtc_lock self-custody with digest-bound cbtcBackingSats (10th BitcoinReflectionPublicValues field; the off-pool CbtcBuffer reads it). Lineage: settle 0x00bb82ef->0x00c11f48->0x009cb098->0x0026dabb(AMM)->0x00d5b572(cBTC); reflection 0x0099e1c7->0x00687472->0x00afe060->0x002d2536(ModeB)->0x004d8dbd(AMM)->0x005e6adc(cBTC)->0x007a9fee(burn-deposit+cmint+CBURN+mode_b gate). BITCOIN_RELAY_VKEY is now 0x007a9fee (forward bridge); the verify_sp1_proof gate means forward batches prove without Mode-B, reverse-bridge (mode_b=1) still needs the eth-reflection inner proof (B1). Both reflection *ProofReal verify on-chain at 0x007a9fee. Settle 0x00d5b572 unchanged.
